recipes.title: Fish and Chips
recipes.description: British classic of beer-battered cod with thick-cut chips and mushy peas.
steps: Cut potatoes into thick chips, rinse and pat dry.
Fry chips at 140°C for 8 minutes; drain.
Whisk flour, cornflour, baking powder and beer to a smooth batter; rest 10 minutes.
Heat oil to 180°C.
Pat fish dry, dust in flour, dip in batter and lower into oil.
Fry fish 6-7 minutes until golden and crisp; drain.
Refry chips at 190°C for 3 minutes until crisp.
Season with salt and serve with malt vinegar.

recipes.title: Shepherd's Pie
recipes.description: British minced lamb pie topped with golden mashed potato.
steps: Sauté chopped onion, carrot and garlic in oil until soft.
Add lamb and brown well, then stir in tomato paste, Worcestershire, thyme and stock.
Simmer 25 minutes until thick; season.
Meanwhile boil potatoes until tender; mash with butter and milk.
Spread lamb in a baking dish and top evenly with mash, fluffing with a fork.
Sprinkle cheddar over the top.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden and bubbling.

recipes.title: Bangers and Mash
recipes.description: British pork sausages with creamy mash and rich onion gravy.
steps: Boil peeled potatoes in salted water until tender.
Pan-fry sausages in oil over medium heat 15 minutes, turning often, until cooked through.
Slice onions and cook slowly in the sausage pan until deeply caramelised.
Stir flour into onions, then add stock and Worcestershire; simmer to a glossy gravy.
Mash potatoes with butter and milk until smooth; season.
Serve sausages on a pile of mash, smothered with onion gravy.

recipes.title: Yorkshire Pudding
recipes.description: Crisp, billowing British batter pudding traditionally served with roast beef.
steps: Whisk flour, salt, eggs and milk to a smooth lump-free batter; rest 30 minutes.
Place a teaspoon of dripping in each hole of a 12-hole muffin tin.
Heat tin in a 220°C oven until fat is smoking hot.
Pour batter quickly into each hole, filling halfway.
Bake 20-25 minutes without opening the oven until risen and deep golden.
Serve immediately with roast beef and gravy.

recipes.title: Cornish Pasty
recipes.description: Cornish hand pie of beef, swede, potato and onion in a sturdy pastry crust.
steps: Rub lard and butter into flour and salt until breadcrumb-like.
Add cold water and bring together; rest dough 30 minutes in fridge.
Dice beef, potato, swede and onion small (1cm).
Roll pastry and cut six 20cm discs.
Pile filling on each disc, season generously, dot with butter.
Brush edges with egg, fold and crimp into a rope along the top.
Brush with beaten egg and bake at 220°C for 15 minutes, then 180°C for 45 minutes.

recipes.title: Toad in the Hole
recipes.description: British sausages baked in fluffy Yorkshire pudding batter, served with onion gravy.
steps: Whisk flour, salt, eggs and milk to a smooth batter; rest 30 minutes.
Place dripping in a heavy roasting tin and heat at 220°C until smoking.
Add sausages and roast 10 minutes, turning, until browned.
Quickly pour batter around sausages and scatter thyme.
Bake undisturbed 25-30 minutes until risen, deep golden and crisp.
Serve immediately with onion gravy.

recipes.title: Cottage Pie
recipes.description: British minced beef pie blanketed in golden mashed potato.
steps: Sauté chopped onion, carrot, celery and garlic in oil until soft.
Add beef and brown well, then stir in tomato paste, Worcestershire, thyme and stock.
Simmer 25 minutes until thick; season.
Boil potatoes until tender; mash with butter and milk.
Spread beef in a baking dish and top with mash, scoring with a fork.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until top is golden and crisp.

recipes.title: Beef Wellington
recipes.description: Tender beef fillet wrapped in mushroom duxelles, prosciutto and golden puff pastry.
steps: Season beef and sear hard in olive oil on all sides; cool, then brush with mustard.
Pulse mushrooms with shallots, garlic and thyme; cook in butter until very dry; cool.
Lay prosciutto on cling film overlapping; spread mushroom layer over.
Place beef on top and roll tightly using the cling film; chill 30 minutes.
Roll puff pastry, unwrap beef and place on pastry; wrap and seal edges.
Score the top, brush with egg yolk and chill 30 minutes.
Bake at 200°C for 35-40 minutes until golden and beef is medium-rare.
Rest 15 minutes before slicing thickly.

recipes.title: Eton Mess
recipes.description: British dessert of crushed meringue, whipped cream and macerated strawberries.
steps: Hull and quarter strawberries; toss with sugar and rest 30 minutes.
Crush a third of the strawberries with a fork to release juice.
Whip cream with vanilla and icing sugar to soft peaks.
Crush meringues into bite-size pieces.
Fold strawberries and meringues into the cream loosely so streaks remain.
Spoon into glasses and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Sticky Toffee Pudding
recipes.description: Moist British date sponge soaked in warm toffee sauce.
steps: Pour boiling water over dates with bicarbonate of soda; rest 10 minutes, then mash.
Cream butter and sugar until pale; beat in eggs one at a time.
Fold in flour, vanilla and the date mixture.
Pour into a buttered dish and bake at 180°C for 30 minutes until risen and set.
Make sauce: simmer cream, brown sugar and butter 5 minutes until glossy.
Pierce sponge all over and pour over half the sauce; let it absorb.
Serve warm with extra sauce and clotted cream.

recipes.title: Spotted Dick
recipes.description: Traditional British steamed suet pudding studded with currants, served with custard.
steps: Combine flour, suet, sugar, currants, zest and salt in a bowl.
Stir in milk to form a soft dough.
Shape into a log and wrap in pleated buttered parchment, then foil; tie ends like a cracker.
Place in a steamer over simmering water and steam 90 minutes, topping up water as needed.
Unwrap carefully, slice thickly and serve with hot custard.

recipes.title: Trifle
recipes.description: British layered dessert of sponge, jelly, fruit, custard and whipped cream.
steps: Cube sponge and place in a glass trifle bowl; drizzle with sherry.
Scatter raspberries over the sponge.
Dissolve jelly in boiling water, cool slightly and pour over fruit; chill until set.
Pour cold custard over the set jelly in an even layer.
Whip cream with icing sugar to soft peaks and spread over the custard.
Toast almonds and scatter on top; chill at least 2 hours before serving.

recipes.title: Scones
recipes.description: British baked scones with a tender crumb, served warm with clotted cream and jam.
steps: Rub cold butter into flour, baking powder and salt until breadcrumb-like.
Stir in sugar, then add most of the milk to bring together a soft dough.
Pat out to 2.5cm thick and stamp out 6cm rounds.
Place on a tray and brush tops with beaten egg.
Bake at 220°C for 12-15 minutes until risen and golden.
Cool on a rack and serve warm with clotted cream and jam.

recipes.title: Black Pudding
recipes.description: British blood sausage with oats and warm spices, sliced and pan-fried until crisp.
steps: Sauté finely chopped onions in a little fat until soft; cool.
Cube pork fat finely and combine with oatmeal, milk, onions, blood and spices.
Stuff loosely into rinsed casings, leaving room for oats to swell.
Tie into 20cm sausages.
Poach gently in 80°C water 30 minutes; do not boil.
Cool fully, then slice and pan-fry in a little oil until crisp on both sides.

recipes.title: Lancashire Hotpot
recipes.description: Slow-cooked lamb stew topped with overlapping potato slices crisped in the oven.
steps: Cube lamb and toss with seasoned flour; brown in a casserole.
Add sliced onions and carrots; cook 5 minutes.
Stir in stock, Worcestershire, thyme and bay; bring to a simmer.
Layer kidney slices on top, then overlap thinly sliced potatoes to cover the surface.
Brush potatoes with melted butter and season.
Cover and bake at 160°C for 2 hours.
Uncover and bake another 30 minutes until potatoes are golden and crisp.

recipes.title: Treacle Tart
recipes.description: British shortcrust tart filled with golden syrup, breadcrumbs and lemon.
steps: Rub butter into flour and sugar, add egg and water, bring to a dough; rest 30 minutes.
Roll pastry and line a 23cm tart tin; chill 15 minutes.
Blind bake at 180°C for 15 minutes with beans, then 5 minutes empty.
Warm golden syrup gently, then stir in breadcrumbs, lemon zest, juice and cream.
Pour filling into the case and smooth the top.
Bake at 170°C for 25-30 minutes until set and golden.
Cool to warm and serve with cream or custard.
